metadata.title: AI Replacing Jobs: Check Your Role's Risk
metadata.description: Is AI replacing jobs? Enter your job title and daily tasks to estimate AI exposure, see exposed tasks, and get a practical career survival plan.

page.title: AI Replacing Jobs: Check Your Role's Risk

hero.title: AI Replacing Jobs: Check Your Role's Risk
hero.description: AI is already changing how people write, code, analyze data, answer customers, and manage routine work. Use this free checker to estimate how exposed your own job is and what you can do next.
hero.tip: This is not a layoff prediction. It estimates AI exposure from your job title, daily tasks, and work style.

checker.label: Free AI job risk checker
checker.title: Estimate your AI exposure from real daily tasks
checker.description: A job title alone is too shallow. Select what you actually do each week to see a task-level AI exposure estimate.

exposed.title: Jobs Most Exposed to AI
exposed.description: Jobs with repetitive, text-based, data-heavy, or rules-based tasks tend to be more exposed to AI automation.

items.title: Customer support
items.description: FAQ responses, routing, and ticket summaries are increasingly handled by AI assistants.

items.title: Routine writing
items.description: Drafting, rewriting, headlines, and first-pass SEO content are exposed to AI generation.

items.title: Data and spreadsheets
items.description: Structured cleanup, formula help, and routine summaries can be accelerated by AI.

items.title: Junior digital work
items.description: Entry-level screen work often contains more repeatable production tasks.

safer.title: Jobs Less Likely to Be Fully Replaced
safer.description: Roles are generally more resilient when they depend on physical presence, human trust, care, emergency response, leadership, or accountability.

items.title: Hands-on work
items.description: Skilled trades, site diagnosis, and physical repair are less exposed to software-only AI.

items.title: Care and trust
items.description: Healthcare, teaching, and coaching require motivation, empathy, and human relationship.

items.title: Accountability
items.description: Legal, safety, compliance, and leadership decisions need responsible ownership.

statistics.title: AI Replacing Jobs Statistics Need Context
statistics.description: Reports measure different things: jobs displaced, jobs created, work hours automated, and task-level exposure are not the same metric.

items.title: Exposure is not the same as job loss
items.description: A role can be highly exposed because AI changes tasks, while still needing human judgment.

items.title: New work can appear too
items.description: Labor-market forecasts include displacement, creation, and transition pressure.

items.title: Your task mix matters
items.description: The same title can look safer or riskier depending on daily responsibilities.

items.title: Adaptation lowers risk
items.description: Workers who learn AI workflows can often move toward oversight and higher-value tasks.

method.title: How the AI Exposure Score Works
method.description: The checker uses transparent rules. It does not call an AI provider and does not save your input.

items.title: Start with a job baseline
items.description: Known occupations have a conservative baseline score based on task exposure.

items.title: Add your task mix
items.description: Digital, repeatable, text-heavy, and data-heavy tasks increase exposure.

items.title: Adjust for work style
items.description: Physical presence, care, leadership, and accountability reduce replacement risk.

items.title: Turn the score into action
items.description: The result includes exposed tasks, protected tasks, skills to learn, and a 90-day plan.

items.question: Is AI really replacing jobs?
items.answer: AI is already changing many tasks, especially writing, coding, customer support, research, and data work. But replacing a task is not the same as replacing an entire job.

items.question: Is this a job loss prediction?
items.answer: No. This tool estimates AI exposure based on your role and daily tasks. It does not predict layoffs or guarantee that a job will disappear.

items.question: What jobs are safest from AI?
items.answer: Jobs that require physical presence, in-person trust, care work, emergency response, leadership, or high-stakes accountability are generally less likely to be fully replaced.

items.question: How is the score calculated?
items.answer: The score combines a job baseline, selected daily tasks, work type, experience level, and AI usage level. The v1 model is deterministic and transparent.

cta.title: Check your job before guessing your risk
cta.description: A headline about AI jobs is not enough. Your daily tasks are the real signal.

buttons.title: Open the AI Job Risk Checker
